channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The end of an era? TSMC, Broadcom could tear apart Intel's legendary business after 57 years by separating its foundry and chip design
item.description: Broadcom and TSMC reportedly looking to chop Intel in half, with separate interests in its foundry and chip design.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: How to set your preferred web browser on your desktop and mobile devices
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy takes you through the steps to easily set your preferred web browser whether you're using a Mac, Windows PC, iPhone or Android.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Wikipedia became a political lightning rod
item.description: Wikipedia has faced political threats for years, but this time, it may be at a breaking point. Republicans have ramped up attacks against Wikipedia as yet another “woke” institution.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Leaked Nothing Phone 3a and 3a Pro renders tease a mid-range phone that should have Samsung worried
item.description: Thanks to a leaked render, we could have our first proper look at the Nothing Phone 3a and 3a Pro – and even by looks alone, they seem to be worthy challengers for Samsung’s mid-range Galaxy handsets.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: The most powerful Rolls-Royce in history is electric and it’s here to boost performance –and the marque's luxury appeal
item.description: Rolls-Royce's debut electric vehicle gets the Black Badge treatment, with 'murdered out' styling and a hefty dose of extra power.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia's RTX 5000 GPUs continue to face severe supply issues, with RTX 5070 Ti reportedly being even worse for launch stock than the RTX 5080
item.description: Nvidia's RTX 5000 GPU launch is getting worse every day, it seems, as new reports indicate the RTX 5070 Ti is facing dire supply shortages.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Birmingham City Council's ERP Project: A Cautionary Tale of Governance Failures
item.description: Birmingham City Council's troubled enterprise resource planning system has become a case study of how large-scale IT projects can go awry.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI humanoid robots inch their way toward the workforce
item.description: AI-based humanoid robots could soon start becoming a workforce reality, thanks to parallel advances in generative AI and electromechanical components and mechatronics, enabling robots to have reasoning skills as well as physical abilities that exceed their predecessors.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump’s tariffs on China could risk access to rare earth elements widely used in tech sector
item.description: Lithium and other elements that the U.S. needs for vital defense, energy, and other applications could become harder to obtain if the Trump administration goes ahead with tariffs and other protectionist policies aimed at China.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Quantum computing breakthrough? Microsoft says its new Majorana 1 chip shows we’re closer than ever
item.description: Microsoft on Wednesday unveiled a new chip that it said showed quantum computing is “years, not decades” away, joining Google and IBM in predicting that a fundamental change in computing technology is much closer than recently believed.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: OSINT Unveiled: The Technology Behind the Intelligence – ODNI Edition
item.description: Learn how OSINT is shaping the future of intelligence and security. The post OSINT Unveiled: The Technology Behind the Intelligence – ODNI Edition first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Bannon: ‘Oligarchs’ will abandon Republicans
item.description: Steve Bannon argued that the “oligarchs” close to President Trump will abandon Republicans the same way they did to Democrats. Bannon was briefly interviewed by CNN’s Donie O’Sullivan on Wednesday, when he continued his case against tech billionaires Elon Musk and Mark Zuckerberg.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft’s first step to scalable quantum computing
item.description: There’s a race going on. Not the one to build artificial general intelligence, whatever that may be, but to deliver the first at-scale quantum computer that’s able to solve a class of massive problems that would take far too long using even the most powerful supercomputer.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Lead CMMC architect returns to Pentagon as DoD CISO
item.description: Katie Arrington's return to DoD comes as her lawyers work out a settlement with the government related to the suspension of her security clearance in 2021. The post Lead CMMC architect returns to Pentagon as DoD CISO first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple unveils a souped-up and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone
item.description: Apple has released a sleeker and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone in an attempt to widen the audience for a bundle of artificial intelligence technology that the company has been hoping will revive demand for its most profitable product lineup.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Elon Musk shared Mars plan over drinks, Star Trek convo: Entrepreneur
item.description: "I realized he was dead serious. Elon Musk doesn't just dream, he makes things happen," said entrepreneur and academic Vivek Wadhwa.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: What Elon Musk did before DOGE
item.description: Elon Musk may be the face of DOGE, but he made a name for himself in businesses built around technology and space travel.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation
